chunk_summary: Guidelines for feeding and fluid management in infants and young children during diarrhea, fever, and respiratory illness, emphasizing breastfeeding, ORS, zinc supplementation, and culturally sensitive advice to prevent dehydration, weight loss, and complications.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of continued breastfeeding and age-appropriate feeding during illness.
Learn the management of diarrhea with ORS, zinc supplementation, and dietary adjustments.
Recognize feeding adaptations for fever and respiratory illness to prevent dehydration and malnutrition.
Address cultural misconceptions about food restriction during illness.
key_facts: Breastfeeding should continue during illness, providing fluids and immune protection.
During diarrhea, give ORS after each loose stool and prescribe zinc for 14 days.
Transient lactose intolerance may occur; consider lactose-free formula only under medical supervision.
During fever, encourage frequent fluids and energy-dense foods; avoid fasting.
For respiratory illness, use saline drops and feed in an upright position to reduce fatigue.
Vomiting requires small, frequent sips and continued breastfeeding to prevent hypoglycemia.
reference_quote: Give ORS after each loose stool and prescribe zinc for 14 days. — World Health Organization, Zinc supplementation in diarrhoea.
document_key_topics: Feeding during diarrhea and transient lactose intolerance
Fluid and energy management during fever
Cultural practices and misconceptions in feeding during illness
